name: Austerlitz: Napoleon's Greatest Victory — Tier 0 free play (no rules enforced)

grid.provenance: hexgrid-direct (flat-top mapping validated on Westwall/Arnhem); module numbering: first=H hType=N vType=N hOff=8 vOff=-1 stagger=false — hex LABELS unverified, geometry is what matters for Tier 0

sides.note: Counter sheets are army sheets: Sheet 1 = French (blue infantry, verified visually), Sheet 2 = Allied (Russian names Bagration/Vorpatzki, verified visually). Stragglers fall to default Allied.

stats.provenance: UNVERIFIED placeholder MA for free-play highlighting only — no rules learned (Tier 0)

movement.note: Tier 0: uniform 1 MP, no ZOC, no terrain — piece pushing, not rules

facing.note: 12 facings [6.1]: even=hexside (Column family), odd=vertex (Line family); matches the module's rotate;12 trait

formations.cite: TEC [5.0] + Stacking Chart [7.1], transcribed cell-by-cell from GMT's updated charts PDF (living_rules/auscharts.pdf, errata applied by GMT 2000-07-20), verified against the rendered page image; formation properties from rulebook 6.3.1-6.3.8

line.cite: 6.3.1/6.3.5: vertex facing; no Road/Strategic Movement; slide via flank 2x if unoccupied; reverse = whole MA; facing change beyond 1st vertex per activation = disorder check each

column.cite: 6.3.2/6.3.6: hexside facing; road+strategic OK; reverse 2x unoccupied; unlimited facing changes at cost

disorder.cite: 6.4.1 + TEC 'na': disordered units have no flank hex (Fig K)

square.cite: 6.3.4: may not move; all-around facing; pays 2 MPs to change formation (TEC note S)

skirmish.cite: 6.3.3: all-around facing, facing changes free; moves through/is moved through at no penalty; only skirmish-capable units

limbered.cite: 6.3.7: may not fire; never moves adjacent to unrouted enemy infantry/cavalry

unlimbered.cite: 6.3.8: may not move into another hex; may change facing one vertex per activation

rows.deep_stream_hexside: No units may cross a Deep Stream hexside except at a Bridge
rows.bridge_hexside: Only units utilizing Road Movement may benefit from bridges (no additional cost)
rows.pond: No units may enter an all-Pond hex

combat_effects.note: TEC right-hand columns (Fire shift / Melee DRM / Cav Charge allowed / Defensive terrain) — transcribed now, ENFORCED IN PHASE 2, unused at Tier 1

stacking.cite: Stacking Chart [7.1]: per-hex SP limits; same type+formation+division only; overstack during movement disorders both; leaders free; multi-hex counters may not stack; artillery alone = 2 batteries, with inf/cav = 1 battery; strategic movement = 8 SP inf / 7 SP cav in column

combat_tables.cite: Transcribed from GMT updated charts p3 (auscharts.pdf, 170dpi render) and verified against the module's original printed chart scan; Italian translation cross-checked (its Fire Table class-c row 'C-D' is a translation typo — both English printings read 'C'). PHASE 2 DATA — fire combat not yet enforced.

fire_table_columns.note: Fire Rating letter (adjusted) x defense class -> fire column 1-8 [8.1.8]; cells verbatim, column: letters

fire_results.note: die 0-9 x fire column 1-8 [8.1.8]; M+1/M/M-1 = morale check w/ DRM; integer = SP loss + morale check modified +(n-1); NE = no effect; verified vs Italian translation cell-for-cell

fire_column_modifiers.terrain: columns RIGHT per TEC fire column (1R/2R)

artillery_range.note: hex range -> fire value adjustment [8.1.6]; bands: grapeshot 1-2 all guns; out of range beyond last listed hex

morale_levels.note: printed morale -> Shaken -> Unsteady -> Routed; module models these as marker layers on every unit [9.2]

fatigue_effects.note: army fatigue level -> effects [13.0]; levels not cumulative

melee.cite: PHASE 4 DATA. Charts p4 (auscharts.pdf, 170dpi render) THREE-source verified cell-for-cell vs the GMT-sanctioned Spanish translation (pp.42-43) and the Italian translation (austables4_it) - zero disagreements. Charge ranges/bonuses from playbook A8.1.1-A8.1.2 (Spanish translation; chart panel itself cites [A8.2]; the rulebook's 8.4.1 cross-reference 'A13.1' is a misprint - see source_defects). GMT official errata 2000-07-20 defines the Retreat result and adds the declined-countercharge defender check; David Fox Q&A rulings (grognard.com) fill procedure gaps.

steps.note: Melee Combat Steps panel (charts p4) - step numbers per combat type; errata 2000-07-20 adds to the Charge column: a charged cavalry stack that declines to Countercharge must make a Defender Pre-Melee Morale Check before Melee
charge: 2 Charge Movement (NO pre-melee check for normal charges; Reaction Charges DO check - Fox Q&A)
3 Counter-Charge (Pre-Melee Morale Check; decliners still take a Defender Pre-Melee Check - errata)
4a Formation Check (Square Reaction Check) or 4b Stand (Pre-Melee Morale Check)
7 Melee Continues (rulebook says 'repeat step 5' - misprint for step 6, Fox Q&A; no Charge Bonus)

result_table.note: modified d10 -> loser / SPs lost / morale / other [8.5]. 'Retreat' = an Unsteady Retreat per errata 2000-07-20 (same rules and restrictions as 10.0), performed IN ADDITION to any retreat required by the morale result; the errata's worked example is the validator's gold case. The earlier Fox Q&A answer (retreat = one hex per morale level lost) is SUPERSEDED by the errata - see source_defects.

drm.note: attacker-perspective signs as printed (high roll favors the attacker); 'melee continues' rounds lose the Charge Bonus [8.5.3]
drm.fatigue: against the fatigued side per Fatigue Effects [13.3]
drm.charge_bonus: + charge bonus of charging/reaction-charging/countercharging cavalry; HALVED (round up) vs Square; HALVED (round up) if charger in Column [8.4.2#6]; countercharge = attacker bonus MINUS countercharger bonus, may go negative (Fox Q&A)
drm.multi_hex_mixed_formations: defender formation most favorable to the DEFENDER when a multi-hex charge hits several defenders (errata 6.5.6); if attacker adjacent to front+flank (or flank+rear) of one defender, attacker picks the most advantageous aspect (Fox Q&A)

size_ratio_drm.note: attacker:defender SP ratio, fractions rounded down [8.3.1#5]; charging cavalry in Column counts one-third of its SPs (rounded down); artillery SPs never count

pre_shock.note: Pre-Shock (Pre-Melee) Morale Check Table [8.2, 8.3, 8.4]; d10 + DRM vs morale rating

attacker_results.above_4_plus: may NOT melee, disordered, -1 morale level

defender_results.above_1_2: lose 1 SP, disordered, retreat, -1 morale level
defender_results.above_3_5: lose 2 SPs, disordered, retreat, -2 morale levels

pre_shock.support_rule: a supporting stack must be active, adjacent to the defender, NOT itself bayonet/assault attacking, may support only one attack per activation [8.2.1#2/8.3.1#2], and must have the defender in its front hexsides (Fox Q&A clarification)

form_square.note: Forming Square in Reaction [8.4.2 #4]; d10 + DRM vs morale rating; only the top infantry unit of the target stack checks; the charging cavalry closes to adjacent whether or not the square forms; strategic-movement units may NOT form square [5.2.1]; artillery alone must Stand

results.above_3_plus: square NOT formed, unit disordered, -1 morale level

pursuit.note: Pursuit Table [8.4.2 #8]; rolled when units opposing a charge rout; pursuing cavalry follows the rout path, eliminates 1 SP of the routing unit per hex entered, then is Disordered and Blown-2; pursuers entering enemy Reaction Zones may be Reacted to normally but may not Countercharge if charged

charge.cite: playbook A8.1.1 (charge range) + A8.1.2 (charge bonus), GMT-sanctioned Spanish translation; charge legality 8.4/8.4.1 + 5.1.2 (May Charge marker)

range.heavy_cuirassier_at_2: may charge but loses the Charge Bonus in the melee
range.adjacent: may never charge an adjacent unit
range.inactive_cavalry: Charge Range of inactive (reaction/counter) cavalry = its Reaction Zone [8.4.1]

charge.may_charge_marker: only cavalry that moved no more than HALF its MA (rounded down) this activation may charge; charges resolve after all movement and Offensive Fire [5.1.2, 8.4]
charge.requirements: non-blown cavalry, Full Activation, LOS to target at declaration, terrain chargeable per TEC cav-charge column along the whole path, target stack charged at most once per activation [8.4.1-8.4.2]

blown.blown_2: half MA (rounded up), may not charge, always disordered [8.4.4]
blown.blown_1: half MA (rounded up), may not charge, may change formation [8.4.4]
blown.recovery: cavalry that neither moves nor defends in any combat (except medium/long-range artillery fire) during a turn gets a Recovery marker after its division's activation; in the Rally Phase remove Recovery markers and reduce Blown level by one [8.4.5]

policy_ai.note: Tier-3: transparent policy AI, submits only through the gate; multi-seed AI-vs-AI campaigns complete the A15.1 scenario (victory or the 4:40pm turn limit) and replay byte-exact through verify_game. Stateless per decision - it answers exactly the windows it owns (reaction/shock/return-fire), so a human opponent keeps every decision of their own. Declared-weak: no melee supports [8.2.1#2], no strategic movement declarations [5.2], no reaction limber [6.2.5], no countercharge play, no multi-turn plans.

game.title: Austerlitz: Napoleon's Greatest Victory (Great Battles of the Napoleonic Wars, Vol. I)
game.art: Mark Simonitch (map art); Rodger B. MacGowan (counter art)

module.title: Austerlitz 1.0.0 (VASSAL module)
module.contributors: Mark Simonitch (original art for counters and maps, per library credits)
module.source: the module's own readme + the module library listing

credits.note: All game content remains the property of GMT Games; all module art and data remain the work of the module author above. Bring-your-own-module: the module is downloaded by each user from the VASSAL module library, never redistributed here. Enforcement tier and rules scope: see the scenario's rules_scope block.

rules_docs.note: GMT hosts these free; local copies in literature/austerlitz (gitignored).

command.cite: 4.0-4.8 Sequence of Play & Command System; charts p1 (Command Change [4.2], Command Breakdown [4.7], Independent Activation [A4.3.2], Command Range [A4.2], Sequence of Play [3.0]) cross-verified: chart text layer vs 170dpi render vs GMT sanctioned Spanish translation A4.x; designer rulings from David Fox Q&A (grognard.com); GMT official errata 2000-07-20

phases: A. Pool Placement: Command Change [4.2] -> Initiative Determination [4.4] -> Initiative Choice -> Pool Placement
B. LIM Activation: Initiative Activation [4.5] -> LIM Selection (repeat until pool empty)
C. Non-LIM: Initiative player and non-initiative player alternate Limited Activations of divisions without LIMs in the pool or single Out of Command units -> LIM Removal
D. Rally: Rally [12.0] -> Rout Loss -> Cavalry Reform -> Fatigue [13.0]

command_change.cite: 4.2 + Command Change Table (charts p1); one attempt per player per turn, normally by the Overall Commander; NOT allowed on turn 1 (designer Q&A: initial LIMs are the starting orders)

effects_key.corps1_or_div3: Add 1 Corps LIM or 3 Division LIMs
effects_key.corps1_or_div2: Add 1 Corps LIM or 2 Division LIMs

notes: dagger: add one to the die when Buxhowden is rolling for the change (kept secret per errata 2000-07-20 to rule 4.2)
asterisk (French 0/2, Allied 1): in scenario A18.4 treat as the next higher number
corps LIM only if corps commander within Overall Commander's range; adding a corps LIM removes that corps' division LIMs from the pool and bars them while it remains [4.2.1]
division LIM only if division commander within his corps commander's range [4.2.2]
additions are not revealed; a player may never examine the pool (errata 2000-07-20 to 4.2)

command_breakdown.cite: 4.7 + Command Breakdown Table (charts p1); column = leader personality; rolled when a Full Activation attempt fails [4.5.1]

results_key.stop: Commander finished for the turn; no In Command unit of the division may activate this turn [4.7]
results_key.retreat: If any unit of the division is within 3 hexes of an enemy combat unit, all In Command units must attempt to retreat half MA (rounded up) away from the enemy (unlimbered artillery limbers and moves); may not move closer to any other enemy. Leader finished either way [4.7]
results_key.limited: Division may undertake a Limited Activation [4.6.2]
results_key.full: Division may undertake a Full Activation [4.6.1]
results_key.charge: If any unit of the division is within 4 hexes of an enemy combat unit the entire division must Charge (cavalry) or advance and melee the closest enemy; otherwise treat as FULL [4.7] (melee = phase 4; no Aggressive leader exists in the Northern Flank scenario, so this result is unreachable there)
results_key.reactivate: Full Activation for the rolling division, then the SAME player may attempt to activate his closest friendly division, even if already activated this turn (no division reactivates twice per turn; Reactivate twice in a row = FULL) [4.7]; the opportunity is optional (designer Q&A) and does not add fatigue (designer Q&A)
results_key.enemy: Rolling commander takes STOP; the ENEMY player may attempt to activate his division closest to the failing commander (even if already activated this turn; no division reactivates twice per turn; Enemy twice in a row = STOP) [4.7]; optional (designer Q&A), no fatigue (designer Q&A)

independent_activation.cite: A4.3/A4.3.2 + Independent Activation Table (charts p1): when a side's Independent LIM is drawn, roll; the result is the total number of that side's Independent leaders that may attempt activation. Leaders above the total use a Limited Activation in the Non-LIM Phase or activate in a Corps Activation. Independent leaders activate once per turn however activated.

command_ranges.cite: A4.2 Command Range Chart (charts p1 = Spanish A4.2, identical); a leader counter's printed range overrides where present [4.1.2]

activations.full: All In Command units of the division: full MA movement, then combat; once one unit of the division fires no more movement this activation [4.6.1]
activations.limited: Automatic, no roll [4.5.1]. Choose: (a) all In Command units of the division move up to HALF MA (rounded down) then may conduct Fire combat, or (b) any ONE Out of Command unit moves up to ONE-THIRD MA (rounded down) then may fire. Limited-activation units may not move adjacent to enemy units - any hexside, per designer Q&A - and may not melee [4.6.2]
activations.in_command: Determined the moment the division activates: units within the division commander's command range are In Command; others are marked Out of Command and must act in the Non-LIM Phase [4.3.3]

fatigue_rules.track: Per division, level 0-9; all combat units except artillery [13.0]
fatigue_rules.increase: +1 in the Fatigue Segment if the division's LIM was in the Command Pool this turn - regardless of what happened ('Any time a division has its LIM in the Pool it gains fatigue, no matter what it actually does during the turn' - designer Q&A; includes STOP results and limited activations; the chosen Initiative LIM counts: it is one of the LIMs committed this turn) - OR if the division performed or was the victim of any combat other than medium/long-range artillery fire [13.1.2]. Max one level per turn [13.1]. Divisions activating under the Independent LIM gain fatigue only if they activate [13.1.1]. Breakdown free activations (Reactivate/Enemy) add none (designer Q&A)
fatigue_rules.decrease: -1 in the Fatigue Segment if ALL of: LIM not in the pool this turn; no combat performed or suffered (other than medium/long-range artillery fire); every unit 3+ hexes from the nearest enemy combat unit [13.2]
fatigue_rules.effects: Fatigue Effects Table (combat_tables.fatigue_effects) [13.3]: morale-check DRM +1 at level 4+; melee DRM at 6+ (phase 4); IMMEDIATE on reaching 7: all division units lose one morale level (one-time, recoverable only by normal Rally - errata 2000-07-20); on reaching 8: disordered; on reaching 9: lose one morale level + disordered

breakpoint.cite: 11.2/11.2.1 + Command Card 'Divisional Breakpoint' roster (Spanish translation of the Command Card); division breakpoint level = number of its combat units at Unit Breakpoint [11.1]
breakpoint.effects: At Division Breakpoint: no Full Activations; LIM withdrawn in the next LIM Removal Segment and may NEVER be re-added; may not activate in a Corps Activation or under the Independent LIM [11.2.1]. Limited Activation in the Non-LIM phase remains legal.

source_defects.defect: Command Card lists Suchet as '2 Div/V' (breakpoint 8) while the counters of his battalions carry division band 3 and the A15.2 setup deploys '3 Div/V' at B1414 alongside '1 Div/V' (Caffarelli) - the card and the counters mix numbers for V Corps' infantry divisions
source_defects.evidence: Command Card roster: 'V Cuerpo-Lannes: 1 Div/V-Caffarelli 8, 2 Div/V-Suchet 8' (sanctioned Spanish translation of the card); counters 1/34 Ln etc. show division band '3'; Spanish A15.2 initial placement: 'A dos hexes o menos de B1414: Toda la 3 Div/V'
source_defects.resolution: Units keep their printed counter band (3) as Suchet's division id; his Division Breakpoint Level is 8 as printed on the card. The label mismatch has no play effect.
source_defects.authority: proven outcome-equivalence (identity of the division is unambiguous; only its number label conflicts)

source_defects.defect: A15.1 gives the Allied player Vorpaizki's LIM, but every Allied infantry battalion in the scenario carries brigade band 2 (Markov); Vorpaizki's own 3rd Brigade (St.P Dragoons, Leib Cuirassiers, Tver Dragoons per A15.2) is not in the scenario, leaving his LIM commanding at most the corps horse battery via the scenario's artillery rule
source_defects.evidence: Counter art: all nine battalions (Arkh/Pskv/O.Ing) show '2', Markov shows '2', Vorpatzki shows '3'; Spanish A15.2 OOB lists all nine under '2a Brigada/Cuerpo de Bagration ... Apilado: Markov'; the module's Northern Flank save nevertheless places Vorpatzki at the head of the Pskov/O.Ingermanland column, hinting the printed playbook page 8 diagram may split the regiments between the two leaders (that page is not in the sanctioned translation, which defers placement to the printed booklet)
source_defects.resolution: Rules-as-written enforced: all nine battalions are Markov's brigade; the a/B horse battery may attach to Markov or Vorpaizki under the A15.1 special artillery rule. Flagged for module-author/expert review against the printed playbook.
source_defects.authority: rules-as-written (counter bands + A15.2 OOB); pending expert review

source_defects.defect: 13.1.1 triggers fatigue when a LIM 'is drawn from the Command Pool', but the Initiative LIM is chosen in the Initiative Choice Segment and never enters the pool [3.0.A.3-4], leaving its fatigue status undefined by the letter of the rule
source_defects.evidence: 3.0 A.3/A.4: the chosen LIM begins the turn; 'All REMAINING available LIMs are placed in an opaque container'; 13.1.1: 'Every time a Division's LIM is drawn from the Command Pool, its Fatigue Level will increase'
source_defects.resolution: The Initiative LIM fatigues its division like any committed LIM
source_defects.authority: designer clarification (David Fox, grognard.com Q&A: 'Any time a division has its LIM in the Pool it gains fatigue, no matter what it actually does during the turn')

source_defects.defect: 8.4.1 sends the reader to 'the playbook, see A13.1' for cavalry Charge Ranges, but the playbook's combat appendix is numbered A8 (A8.1.1 Charge Range, A8.1.2 Charge Bonus); no A13 section exists
source_defects.evidence: Rulebook 8.4.1: 'Active cavalry has a Charge Range usually dependent upon the type of cavalry [as listed in the playbook, see A13.1]'; sanctioned Spanish playbook: 'A8.1.1 Distancia de Carga'; the charts p4 Charge Bonus panel itself cites '[A8.2]'
source_defects.resolution: Charge ranges and bonuses read from playbook A8.1.1/A8.1.2 (max 4 hexes; min 2, or 3 for Heavy/Cuirassier who may charge at 2 without their bonus; never adjacent)
source_defects.authority: proven outcome-equivalence (the referenced content exists uniquely at A8.1.x; only the pointer is wrong)

source_defects.defect: The Melee Result Table's 'Retreat' result is undefined in the printed rules (10.0 only covers Unsteady/Rout retreats), and the two official clarifications CONTRADICT each other: the designer's Q&A answer makes the retreat distance equal to the morale levels lost, while the later official errata makes it a fixed one-hex Unsteady Retreat
source_defects.evidence: Fox Q&A (grognard.com): 'The retreat applies to units not unsteady or routed and is equal in number of hexes to the number of morale levels gained by the melee loser'; GMT errata 2000-07-20: 'Treat the Retreat result as an Unsteady Retreat, covered by the same rules and restrictions as section 10.0, and is performed in addition to any retreat results required by Morale', with a worked example where a roll of 1 (-2 morale levels) retreats exactly 1 hex for the Retreat result
source_defects.resolution: The errata is enforced: Retreat = one Unsteady-style retreat move under 10.0 restrictions, in addition to any morale-driven retreat. The engine's validator replays the errata's own worked example.
source_defects.authority: official errata (2000-07-20) supersedes the earlier informal Q&A ruling

source_defects.defect: 8.4.2 step 7 (Melee Continues) instructs 'repeat step 5', but step 5 of the charge procedure is Return Fire; the melee round to repeat is step 6
source_defects.evidence: Rulebook 8.4.2: '7) Melee Continues. If the result is "melee continues" ... repeat step 5, except that the Charge Bonus no longer applies'; Fox Q&A: 'Q: 8.4.2 7) "then repeat step 5" I guess it is step 6? DF- Step 6 is correct.'
source_defects.resolution: Continued charge melee repeats the Melee step (6) without the Charge Bonus; Return Fire is not repeated
